author: Ben Verbeken
description: This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff. Annotate PDFs with your Apple Pencil. Strokes save to a JSON sidecar; the PDF is never modified.

manifest.author: Ben Verbeken
manifest.description: Annotate PDFs with your Apple Pencil. Strokes save to a JSON sidecar; the PDF is never modified.
manifest.name: Jot

name: Jot

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Fixes from the Obsidian community directory automated review.

- Settings tab no longer recalls the deprecated `display()` API on reset — color picker components are updated in place. The broader migration to `getSettingDefinitions()` is tracked in #1.
- `merge-service.ts`: build the export buffer as a concrete `ArrayBuffer` so `writeBinary` receives the expected type.
- `styles.css`: dropped `clip-path` on the invisible drag-target (corner taps inside the palette region can now also start a drag — minor) and replaced `all: unset` on palette buttons with the specific resets they actually need.

No behavior changes for end users.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Metadata-only release in preparation for the Obsidian community plugin submission.

## Changes

- `manifest.json`: shortened the description so the in-app browser listing reads better.
- `manifest.json`: raised `minAppVersion` to **1.4.4** to match the actual API floor (`addColorPicker` used by the settings tab is `@since 1.4.4`).
- `versions.json`: corrected the `1.0.0 → 1.4.4` mapping for the same reason.

No code changes since 1.0.0.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
First stable release of Jot.

Annotate PDFs in Obsidian with your Apple Pencil. Strokes live in a tiny JSON sidecar (`<file>.jot.json`) next to the original — the PDF is never modified — so your annotations sync via Git or iCloud alongside the rest of your vault.

I built it to mark up musical scores during rehearsal; works equally well for any PDF you want to scribble on.

## What's in 1.0.0

- **Pressure-sensitive ink** with Apple Pencil
- **Three tools**: pen, highlighter, eraser
- **Radial palette** opens on long-press — pick a tool, color, or width without leaving the page. Remembers your last pen and highlighter settings
- **Seven configurable colors** and four widths, settable in *Settings → Jot*
- **Palm rejection** — touch input is ignored once a pen stroke starts; rest your hand freely
- **Per-PDF undo/redo** with a 50-step history
- **Merge notes into PDF** — bake annotations into a flattened PDF (overwrite or save a copy)
- **Clear annotations on this PDF** command, undoable
- **Cross-device sync** — edit on iPad, see it on desktop, via the sidecar JSON
- **Handedness setting** — palette fans away from your pen hand

## Installing

Via [BRAT](https://github.com/TfTHacker/obsidian42-brat): paste `https://github.com/bverbeken/jot` as a beta plugin. Community-store submission is planned.

## Since 0.11.0

- Custom palette colors in settings (seven color pickers + reset)
- Fix: palette downward fan now tilts away from the pen hand correctly
- Internal: refactored into focused single-purpose classes for maintainability — main.ts went from 1100+ lines to under 200, with a full unit test suite (161 tests)
- Sidecar files renamed from `.ink.json` to `.jot.json` (breaking — rename any existing sidecars manually)

releases.author: bverbeken
releases.description:
Three new things since 0.9.0:

- **Persisted tool state**: the plugin remembers the tool, color, and width you last picked across Obsidian sessions. Reopening a PDF resumes where you left off instead of resetting to a default. (Default for fresh installs is black pen, was red.)
- **Per-tool memory**: pen and highlighter each remember their own color and width. Switching pen → highlighter → pen restores the pen's last settings instead of carrying over the highlighter's. Eraser is a passthrough — its toggle doesn't disturb the visible slot values.
- **Clear annotations on this PDF** command (Cmd-P): wipes every stroke on every page of the active PDF. Confirmation modal so a Cmd-P fat-finger can't trash a doc; undo restores per page.

releases.author: bverbeken
releases.description: Pen strokes are now smoothed with quadratic Bézier curves through midpoints — significantly less jagged at fast pen speeds. Pressure-variable width is preserved via 6-step subdivision per curve. The smoothing applies to the overlay (overlay redraws on pointerup) and to the PDF merge output, so what you see and what you export match. Highlighter rendering unchanged.

releases.author: bverbeken
releases.description:
Cross-device sync now actually works:

- File-open always re-reads the sidecar (the previous in-memory cache hid external changes; even closing and reopening the PDF showed stale strokes until you restarted Obsidian).
- A vault.on('modify') watcher reloads the sidecar and redraws live when an external sync arrives, so you don't have to close the PDF tab to see strokes drawn on the other device.
- Own writes are suppressed via a 1.5 s timestamp window so a save doesn't trigger a self-reload loop.
- Bonus: loadFromDisk now clears stale entries before repopulating, so an erase on one device propagates correctly instead of leaving the removed strokes in the other device's memory.

releases.author: bverbeken
releases.description: No user-visible changes. Internal refactor that split main.ts into focused modules (strokes, settings, merge). Releasing so installed copies match the current source on both desktop and iPad.

releases.author: bverbeken
releases.description: Long-press now shows a small ring under the press point that fills clockwise over the 300 ms hold window — so the user knows the palette is about to open. Appears for both the pen long-press (at the press point) and the two-finger hold (at the initial centroid). Fades in after 60 ms so quick draw strokes don't flash it.

releases.author: bverbeken
releases.description: Color confirm flash now keeps the swatch color visible — only the check glyph overlays (white on dark colors, black on light). Tool and width confirms stay green-filled as before.

releases.author: bverbeken
releases.description: Picking a tool, color, or width from a sub-arc now flashes a check on the tapped slot and auto-dismisses the palette (~280 ms). Selections feel snappier — no extra tap to close. Respects prefers-reduced-motion.

releases.author: bverbeken
releases.description: When a <name>.annotated.pdf already exists, merge writes <name>.annotated.2.pdf instead (and .3, .4, ...) so repeat merges don't overwrite the previous export.

releases.author: bverbeken
releases.description:
Adds a 'Merge notes into PDF' command (command palette / Cmd-P). On the active PDF, asks whether to save as a new "<name>.annotated.pdf" file or overwrite the original. Pen strokes preserve pressure-variable width; highlighter strokes preserve opacity. On overwrite, the sidecar .ink.json is dropped so future edits start fresh on the now-baked PDF.

Plugin bundle grows from ~16 KB to ~444 KB because pdf-lib ships its full PDF encoder.

releases.author: bverbeken
releases.description: Eraser moves into the tool sub-arc (under the tool slot) alongside pen and highlighter. Main arc is now 5 slots: undo, redo, tool, width, color.

releases.author: bverbeken
releases.description: Adds a second palette trigger: two fingers held still on the canvas for 300 ms open the palette at the centroid of the two fingers. Useful when the pen isn't already in hand. The pen long-press still works.

releases.author: bverbeken
releases.description:
- Finger-drag the palette from anywhere inside it, including on top of a button. Pen still draws; mouse still clicks the buttons normally.
- Close button enlarged to match the tool/eraser slot size.

releases.author: bverbeken
releases.description:
- Drag the palette around by touching/dragging the band background (finger or mouse — pen stays reserved for drawing).
- New close (X) button at the arc center to dismiss the palette.

releases.author: bverbeken
releases.description: Adds undo/redo as the two leftmost slots on the main arc. Per-PDF history, capped at 50 entries. Snapshots a stroke-list state before each pen stroke is finalized and at the start of each eraser gesture (only kept if the gesture actually removed something).

releases.author: bverbeken
releases.description: Switch palette items from <button> to <div role="button"> — iOS Safari's residual button styling was still squishing the color and width swatches into ovals on iPad even after the 0.3.2 CSS resets.

releases.author: bverbeken
releases.description: Fix the palette rendering on iPad: iOS Safari was keeping its native button styling through 'all: unset', which distorted the buttons into ovals and hid the Lucide icons. Explicit CSS resets restore the intended look.

releases.author: bverbeken
releases.description: Loosen long-press jitter tolerance (5 → 15 px) so the palette can actually open on iPad — Apple Pencil sampling jitter was tripping the cancel threshold before the 300 ms timer fired.

releases.author: bverbeken
releases.description:
## Tool palette

- **Long-press** the page (≥300 ms, stationary) to open a radial tool palette.
- **Main arc**: current tool · eraser · current width · current color.
- **Sub-arcs**: tap a slot to fan out the choices for that category.
- **Pen, highlighter, eraser** as drawing tools; highlighter renders semi-transparent with no segment-overlap beading.
- **7 colors, 4 widths** in the sub-arcs.

## Other

- Settings tab with a **right- / left-handed** preference that tilts the palette away from the pen hand.
- Ink sidecar bumped to format **v2** with a per-stroke tool field. v1 files still load (treated as pen strokes) and re-save as v2.

releases.author: bverbeken
releases.description: Fix: line vanished from canvas the instant the pointer/pencil lifted. Removes a redundant redraw on pointerup that was clearing the live-drawn stroke.

releases.author: bverbeken
releases.description: Pressure-sensitive line thickness — strokes now vary in width with Apple Pencil pressure. Existing .ink.json files re-render with variable width automatically; no schema change.
